repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
titles_tesim: John of Damascus, Homily on the Dormition of the Virgin Mary
contents_tesim: Arabic translator: Abba Antonios or ‘Abdallah ibn al-Fadl al-Antaki?
Homily on the Dormition of the Virgin Mary?
John of Damascus, Homily on the Dormition of the Virgin Mary
The undertext appears to contain a homily on, or perhaps the narrative of, the dormition of the Virgin Mary from the Bethlehem tradition

There is a clear divergence between this undertext object and the well-known published versions (cf. Shoemaker 2002). See also, Graf, Geschichte, Vol. 1, pp. 249-251 and his references to Sinai MSS of similar content. Finally, compare Enger 1854, p. 54 with the undertext on fol. 30r
full_text_tesim: Arabic translator: Abba Antonios or ‘Abdallah ibn al-Fadl al-Antaki?

Combination of early Naskh and Maghribi scripts | Same script as UTO Homily on the Rich and the Poor. Large bowls with sweeping curves for a number of descending strokes. Occasionally the stems of letter alif have a club-like extension to the left of their top point, and the loop of letter Ḍād and letter ṣād has no “tooth”. The base stroke of the initial letter ‘ayn is elongated backward to the right. Sporadic diacritical marks
